html:
𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Twenty — Part Four

The Twelve Minor Prophets

HAVAKUK · TZEFANYAH · KHAGAI · ZEKHARIYAH 1-8

4QXII — Dead Sea Scrolls Scroll of the Twelve Prophets

Four Prophets of Covenant Faith and Restoration

The Righteous Shall Live by His Faith — YHWH Will Rejoice Over You With Singing — Not by Might Nor by Power But by My Spirit — Ten Men Shall Take Hold of the Robe of a Yehudi

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

4QXII — Dead Sea Scrolls — Cave 4 Qumran — The Scroll of the Twelve

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

1QpHab — Habakkuk Pesher — Cave 1 Qumran

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

HAVAKUK — THE RIGHTEOUS SHALL LIVE BY HIS FAITH

HAVAKUK — CHAPTER 1 The Burden That Havakuk Saw — How Long O YHWH — I Am Raising Up the Chaldeans — Their Justice and Dignity Go Forth From Themselves — Why Do You Idly Look at Wrong

1 The oracle that Havakuk the prophet saw.

2 O YHWH, how long shall I cry for help, and you will not hear? Or cry to you Violence and you will not save?

3 Why do you make me see iniquity, and why do you idly look at wrong? Destruction and violence are before me. Strife and contention arise.

4 So the Torah is paralyzed, and justice never goes forth. For the wicked surround the righteous. So justice goes forth perverted.

5 Look among the nations, and see. Wonder and be astounded. For I am doing a work in your days that you would not believe if told.

6 For behold, I am raising up the Chaldeans, that bitter and hasty nation, who march through the breadth of the earth, to seize dwellings not their own.

7 They are dreaded and fearsome. Their justice and dignity go forth from themselves.

8 Their horses are swifter than leopards, more fierce than the evening wolves. Their horsemen press proudly on. Their horsemen come from afar. They fly like an eagle swift to devour.

9 They all come for violence, all their faces forward. They gather captives like sand.

10 At kings they scoff, and at rulers they laugh. They laugh at every fortress, for they pile up earth and take it.

11 Then they sweep by like the wind and go on, guilty men, whose own might is their god.

12 Are you not from everlasting, O YHWH my Creator, my Holy One? We shall not die. O YHWH, you have ordained them as a judgment, and you, O Rock, have established them for reproof.

13 You who are of purer eyes than to see evil and cannot look at wrong, why do you idly look at traitors and remain silent when the wicked swallows up the man more righteous than he?

14 You make mankind like the fish of the sea, like crawling things that have no ruler.

15 He brings all of them up with a hook. He drags them out with his net. He gathers them in his dragnet. So he rejoices and is glad.

16 Therefore he sacrifices to his net and makes offerings to his dragnet, for by them he lives in luxury, and his food is rich.

17 Is he then to keep on emptying his net and mercilessly killing nations forever?

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Havakuk in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant dialogue structure of Chapter 1 is unique in the prophetic corpus: the prophet addresses YHWH not with oracles delivered to the people but with a covenant complaint — how long? — followed by YHWH's covenant answer, followed by the prophet's covenant counter-complaint. The covenant conversation between the prophet and YHWH is the text itself.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 12 — are you not from everlasting, O YHWH my Creator, my Holy One? We shall not die. The covenant confidence within the covenant complaint: even while challenging YHWH's covenant action, Havakuk grounds his covenant question in the covenant certainty of YHWH's eternal covenant nature. The covenant complaint is not covenant atheism but covenant intimate covenant protest.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. Verse 13 — you who are of purer eyes than to see evil and cannot look at wrong — why do you idly look at traitors? The covenant theological paradox at the heart of Havakuk's complaint: YHWH's covenant holiness — too pure to look at evil — is being appealed to as the reason YHWH should act. If YHWH cannot tolerate evil, why does he permit the Chaldeans — more wicked than Yehudah — to swallow the relatively more righteous?

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2-4 — how long shall I cry and you will not hear? The covenant prophet's first complaint is internal: Yehudah's own covenant injustice — Torah paralyzed, justice perverted. YHWH's covenant answer — verses 5-11 — is not what Havakuk expected: YHWH is raising up the Chaldeans. The covenant answer to covenant internal injustice is covenant external judgment more severe than the covenant internal problem. Havakuk's second complaint — verses 12-17 — is that the covenant remedy seems worse than the covenant disease.

HAVAKUK — CHAPTER 2 I Will Take My Stand at My Watchpost — Write the Vision — The Righteous Shall Live by His Faith — Five Woes — The Earth Shall Be Filled With the Knowledge of YHWH — YHWH Is in His Holy Temple

1 I will take my stand at my watchpost and station myself on the tower, and look out to see what he will say to me, and what I will answer concerning my complaint.

2 And YHWH answered me and said, Write the vision. Make it plain on tablets, so he may run who reads it.

3 For still the vision awaits its appointed time. It hastens to the end — it will not lie. If it seems slow, wait for it. It will surely come. It will not delay.

4 Behold, his soul is puffed up. It is not upright within him, but the righteous shall live by his faith.

5 Moreover, wine is a traitor, an arrogant man who is never at rest. His greed is as wide as Sheol. Like death he has never enough. He gathers for himself all nations and collects as his own all peoples.

6 Shall not all these take up their taunt against him, with scoffing and riddles for him, and say, Woe to him who heaps up what is not his own — for how long — and loads himself with pledges.

7 Will not your debtors suddenly arise, and those awake who will make you tremble? Then you will be spoil for them.

8 Because you have plundered many nations, all the remnant of the peoples shall plunder you, for the blood of man and violence to the earth, to cities and all who dwell in them.

9 Woe to him who gets evil gain for his house, to set his nest on high, to be safe from the reach of harm.

10 You have devised shame for your house by cutting off many peoples. You have forfeited your life.

11 For the stone will cry out from the wall, and the beam from the woodwork respond.

12 Woe to him who builds a town with blood and founds a city on iniquity.

13 Behold, is it not from YHWH of hosts that peoples labor merely for fire, and nations weary themselves for nothing?

14 For the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the kavod of YHWH as the waters cover the sea.

15 Woe to him who makes his neighbors drink — you pour out your wrath and make them drunk, in order to gaze at their nakedness.

16 You will have your fill of shame instead of glory. Drink, yourself, and show your uncircumcision. The cup in YHWH's right hand will come around to you, and utter shame will come upon your glory.

17 The violence done to Lebanon will overwhelm you, as will the destruction of the beasts that terrified them, for the blood of man and violence to the earth, to cities and all who dwell in them.

18 What profit is an idol when its maker has shaped it, a metal image, a teacher of lies? For its maker trusts in his own creation when he makes speechless idols.

19 Woe to him who says to a wooden thing, Awake. To a silent stone, Arise. Can this teach? Behold, it is overlaid with gold and silver, and there is no breath at all in it.

20 But YHWH is in his holy temple. Let all the earth keep silence before him.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 1QpHab — the Habakkuk Pesher from Cave 1 — is the most extensively preserved Dead Sea Scrolls commentary, providing verse-by-verse covenant interpretation of Chapters 1-2. The Qumran community applied Havakuk's covenant oracle to their own historical situation. Verse 4 — the righteous shall live by his faith — is the most theologically generative verse in Havakuk, preserved exactly in the DSS tradition.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 14 — the earth shall be filled with the knowledge of the kavod of YHWH as the waters cover the sea. The covenant universalism of the covenant knowledge: not a partial or selective covenant disclosure but a total covenant filling — as complete as the water coverage of the sea floor. The covenant knowledge of YHWH's covenant glory will leave no covenant space uncovered.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 4 — the righteous shall live by his faith — emunato — his faithfulness, his covenant trust. The covenant life principle: covenant righteous living is sustained by covenant faithfulness to YHWH, not by covenant circumstances or covenant visible evidence. Havakuk's complaint about YHWH's apparent inaction is answered at its deepest level here: the covenant righteous one lives not by sight but by covenant faithful trust.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 20 — YHWH is in his holy temple. Let all the earth keep silence before him. The covenant doxological climax of the five woes: after cataloging the covenant crimes of the arrogant — heaping, building with blood, making neighbors drunk, idolatry — the covenant answer is not yet another argument but a covenant declaration of location and a covenant command of posture. YHWH is in his covenant temple. The covenant appropriate response to covenant overwhelming evil is not louder covenant argument but covenant reverent silence before the covenant sovereign.

HAVAKUK — CHAPTER 3 A Prayer of Havakuk — YHWH Came From Teman — The Earth Was Filled With His Praise — You Went Out for the Salvation of Your People — Though the Fig Tree Should Not Blossom — I Will Joy in the Creator of My Salvation

1 A prayer of Havakuk the prophet, according to Shigionot.

2 O YHWH, I have heard the report of you, and your work, O YHWH, do I fear. In the midst of the years revive it. In the midst of the years make it known. In wrath remember mercy.

3 Creator came from Teman, and the Holy One from Mount Paran. His splendor covered the heavens, and the earth was full of his praise.

4 His brightness was like the light. Rays flashed from his hand, and there he veiled his power.

5 Before him went pestilence, and plague followed at his heels.

6 He stood and measured the earth. He looked and shook the nations. Then the eternal mountains were scattered. The everlasting hills sank low. His were the everlasting ways.

7 I saw the tents of Kushan in affliction. The curtains of the land of Midyan trembled.

8 Was your wrath against the rivers, O YHWH? Was your anger against the rivers, or your indignation against the sea, when you rode on your horses, on your chariot of salvation?

9 You stripped the sheath from your bow, calling for many arrows. You split the earth with rivers.

10 The mountains saw you and writhed. The raging waters swept on. The deep gave forth its voice. It lifted its hands on high.

11 The sun and moon stood still in their place at the light of your arrows as they sped, at the flash of your glittering spear.

12 You marched through the earth in fury. You threshed the nations in anger.

13 You went out for the salvation of your people, for the salvation of your anointed. You crushed the head of the house of the wicked, laying him bare from thigh to neck.

14 You pierced with his own arrows the heads of his warriors, who came like a whirlwind to scatter me, rejoicing as if to devour the poor in secret.

15 You trampled the sea with your horses, the surging of mighty waters.

16 I hear, and my body trembles. My lips quiver at the sound. Rottenness enters into my bones. My legs tremble beneath me. Yet I will quietly wait for the day of trouble to come upon people who invade us.

17 Though the fig tree should not blossom, nor fruit be on the vines, the produce of the olive fail and the fields yield no food, the flock be cut off from the fold and there be no herd in the stalls,

18 Yet I will rejoice in YHWH. I will take joy in the Creator of my salvation.

19 YHWH, the Sovereign, is my strength. He makes my feet like the deer's. He makes me tread on my high places. To the choirmaster: with stringed instruments.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 3 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The psalm of Chapter 3 is the covenant resolution of the entire book: from covenant complaint — Chapter 1 — through covenant answer — Chapter 2 — to covenant praise — Chapter 3. The covenant theophany hymn of verses 3-15 recounts YHWH's covenant victories in the covenant Exodus tradition, establishing the covenant ground for the covenant confidence of verses 17-19.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 3 — Creator came from Teman, and the Holy One from Mount Paran. The covenant theophanic geography: Teman and Paran are the covenant southern wilderness regions through which YHWH moved during the covenant Exodus and Sinai events. The covenant prayer recalls YHWH's covenant historical interventions as the covenant basis for covenant present confidence.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 3. Verses 17-18 — though the fig tree should not blossom, nor fruit be on the vines, the produce of the olive fail — yet I will rejoice in YHWH. I will take joy in the Creator of my salvation. The covenant declaration of covenant joy decoupled from covenant circumstances: every agricultural covenant blessing is removed in the covenant conditional clause — and the covenant conclusion is covenant rejoicing anyway. Covenant joy is not covenant circumstance-dependent.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — in wrath remember mercy. The covenant summary of the entire book's covenant theological question compressed to three Hebrew words: be-rogez rahem tizkor — in anger compassion remember. Havakuk's complaint has been about YHWH's apparent covenant inaction; his covenant answer has been the covenant oracle of judgment; his covenant prayer concludes with the covenant request that within the covenant wrath, YHWH remember the covenant mercy. The covenant tension of the entire book is held in this single covenant petition.

TZEFANYAH — THE GREAT DAY OF YHWH AND THE SONG OF RESTORATION

TZEFANYAH — CHAPTER 1 I Will Utterly Sweep Everything Away — The Day of YHWH Is Near — A Day of Wrath — Their Silver and Gold Cannot Deliver Them — The Great Day of YHWH

1 The word of YHWH that came to Tzefanyahu the son of Kushi, son of Gedalyahu, son of Amaryahu, son of Hizkiyahu, in the days of Yoshiyahu the son of Amon, king of Yehudah.

2 I will utterly sweep away everything from the face of the earth, declares YHWH.

3 I will sweep away man and beast. I will sweep away the birds of the heavens and the fish of the sea, and the rubble with the wicked. I will cut off mankind from the face of the earth, declares YHWH.

4 I will stretch out my hand against Yehudah and against all the inhabitants of Yerushalayim, and I will cut off from this place the remnant of Ba'al and the name of the idolatrous priests along with the priests,

5 Those who bow down on the rooftops to the host of the heavens, those who bow down and swear to YHWH and yet swear by Malkam,

6 Those who have turned back from following YHWH, who do not seek YHWH or inquire of him.

7 Be silent before the Creator YHWH, for the day of YHWH is near. YHWH has prepared a sacrifice and consecrated his guests.

8 And on the day of YHWH's sacrifice — I will punish the officials and the king's sons and all who array themselves in foreign attire.

9 On that day I will punish everyone who leaps over the threshold, and those who fill their master's house with violence and fraud.

10 On that day, declares YHWH, a cry will be heard from the Fish Gate, a wail from the Second Quarter, a loud crash from the hills.

11 Wail, O inhabitants of the Mortar, for all the traders are no more. All who weigh out silver are cut off.

12 At that time I will search Yerushalayim with lamps, and I will punish the men who are complacent, those who say in their hearts, YHWH will not do good, nor will he do ill.

13 Their goods shall be plundered, and their houses laid waste. Though they build houses, they shall not inhabit them. Though they plant vineyards, they shall not drink wine from them.

14 The great day of YHWH is near, near and hastening fast. The sound of the day of YHWH is bitter. The mighty man cries aloud there.

15 A day of wrath is that day, a day of distress and anguish, a day of ruin and devastation, a day of darkness and gloom, a day of clouds and thick darkness,

16 A day of trumpet blast and battle cry against the fortified cities and against the lofty battlements.

17 I will bring distress on mankind, so that they shall walk like the blind, because they have sinned against YHWH. Their blood shall be poured out like dust, and their flesh like dung.

18 Neither their silver nor their gold shall be able to deliver them on the day of the wrath of YHWH. In the fire of his jealousy, all the earth shall be consumed, for a full and sudden end he will make of all the inhabitants of the earth.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Tzefanyah in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 1 is the most comprehensive and cosmic Day of YHWH oracle in the Twelve: it begins with the reversal of creation itself — verse 2-3, sweeping away man, beast, birds, and fish, reversing the covenant creation order of Bereshit — and moves to the specific covenant judgment of Yehudah and Yerushalayim. The covenant Day is simultaneously cosmic and covenant-particular.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 12 — I will search Yerushalayim with lamps, and I will punish the men who are complacent, those who say in their hearts, YHWH will not do good, nor will he do ill. The covenant indictment of theological indifference: not active covenant rebellion but covenant practical atheism — the conviction that YHWH is covenant irrelevant to daily covenant experience. The covenant comfortable who expect nothing from YHWH receive the covenant searching lamp.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. Verses 14-16 — the great day of YHWH is near — a day of wrath, distress, anguish, ruin, devastation, darkness, gloom, clouds, thick darkness, trumpet blast, battle cry. The covenant catalog of Day of YHWH characteristics is the most extensive in the prophetic corpus: nine paired covenant descriptors accumulate into the covenant overwhelming portrait of the covenant day of YHWH's covenant wrath.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1 — Tzefanyahu the son of Kushi, son of Gedalyahu, son of Amaryahu, son of Hizkiyahu. The covenant genealogy of four generations is unusual in the prophetic introductions and may indicate a connection to the royal Hizkiyahu — making Tzefanyahu a covenant distant relative of the covenant royal house. The name Kushi — Cushite — indicates African lineage within the prophet's family. The covenant prophet speaking the most intense Day of YHWH oracle in the canon comes from the Afro-Asiatic covenant corridor.

TZEFANYAH — CHAPTER 2 Gather Together Before the Decree Takes Effect — Seek YHWH — The Oracle Against the Pelishtim — Against Moav and Ammon — Against Kush — Against Assyria — Nineveh a Desolation

1 Gather together. Yes, gather, O shameless nation,

2 Before the decree takes effect — before the day passes away like chaff — before there comes upon you the burning anger of YHWH, before there comes upon you the day of the anger of YHWH.

3 Seek YHWH, all you humble of the land, who do his just commands. Seek righteousness. Seek humility. Perhaps you may be hidden on the day of the anger of YHWH.

4 For Aza shall be forsaken, and Ashkelon shall become a desolation. Ashdod's people shall be driven out at noon, and Ekron shall be uprooted.

5 Woe to you inhabitants of the seacoast, you nation of the Kerethites. The word of YHWH is against you, O Kena'an, land of the Pelishtim, and I will destroy you until no inhabitant is left.

6 And you, O seacoast, shall be pastures, with meadows for shepherds and folds for flocks.

7 The seacoast shall become the possession of the remnant of the house of Yehudah, on which they shall graze, and in the houses of Ashkelon they shall lie down at evening. For YHWH their Creator will be mindful of them and restore their fortunes.

8 I have heard the taunts of Moav and the revilings of the Ammonites, how they have taunted my people and made boasts against their territory.

9 Therefore, as I live, declares YHWH of hosts, the Creator of Yisra'EL, Moav shall become like Sodom, and the Ammonites like Gomorrah, a land possessed by nettles and salt pits, and a waste forever. The remnant of my people shall plunder them, and the survivors of my nation shall possess them.

10 This shall happen to them in return for their pride, because they taunted and boasted against the people of YHWH of hosts.

11 YHWH will be awesome against them. For he will famish all the gods of the earth, and to him shall bow down, each in its place, all the lands of the nations.

12 You also, O Kushim, shall be slain by my sword.

13 And he will stretch out his hand against the north and destroy Assyria, and he will make Nineveh a desolation, a dry waste like the desert.

14 Herds shall lie down in her midst, all kinds of beasts. Even the owl and the hedgehog shall lodge in her capitals. A voice shall hoot in the window. Devastation will be on the threshold, for her cedar work will be laid bare.

15 This is the exultant city that lived securely, that said in her heart, I am, and there is no one else. What a desolation she has become, a lair for wild beasts. Everyone who passes by her hisses and shakes his fist.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 2 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant universalism of the covenant oracles against the nations — Pelishtim, Moav, Ammon, Kush, Assyria — is framed by the covenant invitation of verses 1-3: seek YHWH, seek righteousness, seek humility. The covenant oracles against the nations are the covenant negative backdrop against which the covenant positive invitation to the covenant humble of the land is issued.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 12 — you also, O Kushim, shall be slain by my sword. The Orit tradition reads the brief oracle against Kush — a single verse — within the broader covenant context of Tzefanyah's own Kushite ancestry. The covenant prophet who descends from the Afro-Asiatic covenant corridor pronounces the covenant word against the same covenant geographical region. The covenant word does not exempt the covenant prophet's own covenant heritage.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 15 — this is the exultant city that lived securely, that said in her heart, I am, and there is no one else. The covenant self-divinization of Nineveh: the covenant claim I am and there is no one else is the exclusive covenant claim of YHWH himself — Yesha'yahu 45:6. Nineveh's covenant pride has reached the point of appropriating YHWH's own covenant self-identification.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 3 — seek YHWH, all you humble of the land. Perhaps you may be hidden on the day of the anger of YHWH. The covenant qualifier — perhaps — is the covenant honest acknowledgment that even covenant faithful seeking does not guarantee covenant exemption from the covenant day's effects. The covenant humble are given covenant hope, not covenant guarantee. The covenant perhaps maintains the covenant sovereignty of YHWH over the covenant outcomes while still making the covenant seeking worthwhile.

TZEFANYAH — CHAPTER 3 Woe to the Rebellious and Defiled City — Her Officials Are Roaring Lions — I Have Cut Off Nations — Therefore Wait for Me — I Will Remove the Proud — I Will Leave a Humble and Lowly People — YHWH Will Rejoice Over You With Gladness

1 Woe to her who is rebellious and defiled, the oppressing city.

2 She listens to no voice. She accepts no correction. She does not trust in YHWH. She does not draw near to her Creator.

3 Her officials within her are roaring lions. Her judges are evening wolves that leave nothing till the morning.

4 Her prophets are fickle, treacherous men. Her priests profane what is holy. They do violence to the Torah.

5 YHWH within her is righteous. He does no injustice. Every morning he shows forth his justice. Each dawn he does not fail. But the unjust knows no shame.

6 I have cut off nations. Their battlements are in ruins. I have laid waste their streets so that no one walks in them. Their cities have been made desolate, without a man, without an inhabitant.

7 I said, Surely you will fear me. You will accept correction. Then your dwelling will not be cut off according to all that I have appointed against you. But all the more they were eager to make all their deeds corrupt.

8 Therefore wait for me, declares YHWH, for the day when I rise up to seize the prey. For my decision is to gather nations, to assemble kingdoms, to pour out upon them my indignation, all my burning anger. For in the fire of my jealousy all the earth shall be consumed.

9 For at that time I will change the speech of the peoples to a pure speech, that all of them may call upon the name of YHWH and serve him with one accord.

10 From beyond the rivers of Kush my worshipers, the daughter of my dispersed ones, shall bring my offering.

11 On that day you shall not be put to shame because of the deeds by which you have rebelled against me. For then I will remove from your midst your proudly exultant ones, and you shall no longer be haughty in my holy mountain.

12 But I will leave in your midst a people humble and lowly. They shall seek refuge in the name of YHWH,

13 Those who are left in Yisra'EL. They shall do no injustice and speak no lies, nor shall there be found in their mouth a deceitful tongue. For they shall graze and lie down, and none shall make them afraid.

14 Sing aloud, O daughter of Tziyon. Shout, O Yisra'EL. Rejoice and exult with all your heart, O daughter of Yerushalayim.

15 YHWH has taken away the judgments against you. He has cleared away your enemies. The King of Yisra'EL, YHWH, is in your midst. You shall never again fear evil.

16 On that day it shall be said to Yerushalayim, Fear not, O Tziyon. Let not your hands grow weak.

17 YHWH your Creator is in your midst, a mighty one who will save. He will rejoice over you with gladness. He will quiet you by his love. He will exult over you with loud singing.

18 I will gather those of you who mourn for the appointed feast, so that you will no longer suffer reproach.

19 Behold, at that time I will deal with all your oppressors. And I will save the lame and gather the outcast, and I will change their shame into praise and renown in all the earth.

20 At that time I will bring you in, at the time when I gather you together. For I will make you renowned and praised among all the peoples of the earth, when I restore your fortunes before your eyes, says YHWH.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 3 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant restoration climax of Tzefanyah — verses 14-20 — is the covenant most joyful passage in the Twelve: the covenant command to sing, shout, and rejoice followed by the covenant declaration that YHWH himself rejoices and sings over the covenant community. The covenant singing of YHWH over his covenant people — verse 17 — is matched only by YHWH's joy in Yesha'yahu 62:5.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 10 — from beyond the rivers of Kush my worshipers, the daughter of my dispersed ones, shall bring my offering. The Orit Ge'ez tradition reads this verse as a covenant promise specifically addressed to the Kushite covenant community: worshipers from beyond the Nile river system will bring covenant offerings to YHWH. The covenant geographic specificity of the northeast African covenant corridor is preserved in the covenant text.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 3. Verse 9 — I will change the speech of the peoples to a pure speech, that all of them may call upon the name of YHWH and serve him with one accord. The covenant reversal of Bavel — Bereshit 11: the confusion of covenant languages that scattered humanity is reversed by the covenant gift of pure speech that unites all peoples in covenant worship of YHWH. The covenant linguistic curse becomes a covenant linguistic blessing.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 17 — YHWH your Creator is in your midst, a mighty one who will save. He will rejoice over you with gladness. He will quiet you by his love. He will exult over you with loud singing. The covenant portrait of YHWH's emotional engagement with the covenant community at covenant restoration: rejoicing, quieting, exulting with singing. The covenant God who was depicted as wrathful judge in Chapter 1 is here the covenant joyful celebrant over the covenant restored people. The covenant wrath and the covenant joy belong to the same covenant God.

KHAGAI — NOT BY MIGHT NOR BY POWER BUT BY MY SPIRIT

KHAGAI — CHAPTER 1 Is It Time for You to Dwell in Paneled Houses — My House Lies in Ruins — Consider Your Ways — You Have Sown Much and Harvested Little — The People Feared YHWH — YHWH Stirred Up the Spirit of Zerubbavel

1 In the second year of Darius the king, in the sixth month, on the first day of the month, the word of YHWH came by the hand of Khagai the prophet to Zerubbavel the son of She'altiel, governor of Yehudah, and to Yehoshua the son of Yehotzadak, the high priest:

2 Thus says YHWH of hosts: This people says the time has not yet come to rebuild the house of YHWH.

3 Then the word of YHWH came by the hand of Khagai the prophet,

4 Is it a time for you yourselves to dwell in your paneled houses, while this house lies in ruins?

5 Now, therefore, thus says YHWH of hosts: Consider your ways.

6 You have sown much, and harvested little. You eat, but you never have enough. You drink, but you never have your fill. You clothe yourselves, but no one is warm. And he who earns wages does so to put them into a bag with holes.

7 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Consider your ways.

8 Go up to the hills and bring wood and build the house, that I may take pleasure in it and that I may be glorified, says YHWH.

9 You looked for much, and behold, it came to little. And when you brought it home, I blew it away. Why? declares YHWH of hosts. Because of my house that lies in ruins, while each of you busies himself with his own house.

10 Therefore the heavens above you have withheld the dew, and the earth has withheld its produce.

11 And I have called for a drought on the land and the hills, on the grain, the new wine, the oil, on what the ground brings forth, on man and beast, and on all their labors.

12 Then Zerubbavel the son of She'altiel, and Yehoshua the son of Yehotzadak, the high priest, with all the remnant of the people, obeyed the voice of YHWH their Creator, and the words of Khagai the prophet, as YHWH their Creator had sent him. And the people feared YHWH.

13 Then Khagai, the messenger of YHWH, spoke to the people with YHWH's message, I am with you, declares YHWH.

14 And YHWH stirred up the spirit of Zerubbavel the son of She'altiel, governor of Yehudah, and the spirit of Yehoshua the son of Yehotzadak, the high priest, and the spirit of all the remnant of the people. And they came and worked on the house of YHWH of hosts, their Creator,

15 On the twenty-fourth day of the month, in the sixth month, in the second year of Darius the king.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Khagai in close agreement with the Masoretic. Khagai is the covenant post-exilic prophet whose covenant ministry is precisely dated — the second year of Darius — and whose covenant effect is immediately documented: the covenant leaders and covenant community obey — verse 12. Khagai is the most immediately effective prophet in the Twelve: the covenant word produces covenant action within three weeks of the first covenant oracle.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 6 — you have sown much, and harvested little. You eat, but you never have enough. You drink, but you never have your fill. The covenant economic analysis of the covenant community's covenant situation: the covenant productivity gap is explained not by agricultural conditions but by covenant priorities. The covenant community's preoccupation with their own paneled houses while the covenant house lies in ruins produces the covenant harvest failure.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. Verse 9 — I blew it away. Why? Because of my house that lies in ruins, while each of you busies himself with his own house. The covenant economic explanation that Khagai makes explicit: the covenant community's covenant self-investment at the expense of the covenant house produces covenant economic covenant frustration — YHWH blows away the results of covenant self-focused effort.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 4 — is it a time for you yourselves to dwell in your paneled houses, while this house lies in ruins? The covenant rhetorical question that is the covenant hinge of the book: the covenant community has invested in covenant personal covenant comfort — paneled houses — while the covenant communal covenant center — the covenant house of YHWH — remains in covenant ruins. The covenant economy of covenant priorities is the covenant diagnostic: where covenant investment goes reveals what the covenant community actually values.

KHAGAI — CHAPTER 2 Who Is Left Among You Who Saw This House in Its Former Glory — I Will Fill This House With Glory — The Latter Glory Greater Than the Former — I Will Shake the Nations — The Signet Ring — I Have Chosen You

1 In the seventh month, on the twenty-first day of the month, the word of YHWH came by the hand of Khagai the prophet:

2 Speak now to Zerubbavel the son of She'altiel, governor of Yehudah, and to Yehoshua the son of Yehotzadak, the high priest, and to all the remnant of the people, and say,

3 Who is left among you who saw this house in its former glory? How do you see it now? Is it not as nothing in your eyes?

4 Yet now be strong, O Zerubbavel, declares YHWH. Be strong, O Yehoshua, son of Yehotzadak, the high priest. Be strong, all you people of the land, declares YHWH. Work, for I am with you, declares YHWH of hosts,

5 According to the covenant that I made with you when you came out of Egypt. My Spirit remains in your midst. Fear not.

6 For thus says YHWH of hosts: Yet once more, in a little while, I will shake the heavens and the earth and the sea and the dry land.

7 And I will shake all nations, so that the treasures of all nations shall come in, and I will fill this house with glory, says YHWH of hosts.

8 The silver is mine, and the gold is mine, declares YHWH of hosts.

9 The latter glory of this house shall be greater than the former, says YHWH of hosts. And in this place I will give peace, declares YHWH of hosts.

10 On the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month, in the second year of Darius, the word of YHWH came by Khagai the prophet:

11 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Ask the priests about the Torah:

12 If someone carries holy meat in the fold of his garment and touches with his fold bread or stew or wine or oil or any kind of food, does it become holy? The priests answered and said, No.

13 Then Khagai said, If someone who is unclean by contact with a dead body touches any of these, does it become unclean? The priests answered and said, It does become unclean.

14 Then Khagai answered and said, So is it with this people, and with this nation before me, declares YHWH, and so with every work of their hands. And what they offer there is unclean.

15 Now then, consider from this day onward. Before stone was placed upon stone in the temple of YHWH,

16 How did you fare? When one came to a heap of twenty measures, there were but ten. When one came to the wine vat to draw fifty measures, there were but twenty.

17 I struck you and all the products of your toil with blight and mildew and hail, yet you did not turn to me, declares YHWH.

18 Consider from this day onward, from the twenty-fourth day of the ninth month. Since the day that the foundation of YHWH's temple was laid, consider.

19 Is the seed yet in the barn? Indeed, the vine, the fig tree, the pomegranate, and the olive tree have yielded nothing. But from this day on I will bless you.

20 The word of YHWH came a second time to Khagai on the twenty-fourth day of the month:

21 Speak to Zerubbavel, governor of Yehudah, saying, I am about to shake the heavens and the earth,

22 And to overthrow the throne of kingdoms. I am about to destroy the strength of the kingdoms of the nations, and overthrow the chariots and their riders. And the horses and their riders shall go down, every one by the sword of his brother.

23 On that day, declares YHWH of hosts, I will take you, O Zerubbavel my servant, the son of She'altiel, declares YHWH, and make you like a signet ring, for I have chosen you, declares YHWH of hosts.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 2 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant encouragement of verses 3-5 addresses the covenant discouragement of those who remember the Solomonic temple's former glory: the second temple appears as nothing by comparison. YHWH's covenant answer is covenant promise — the covenant latter glory will exceed the covenant former — and covenant presence — my Spirit remains in your midst.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 23 — I will make you like a signet ring, for I have chosen you. The covenant reversal of the Yekonyahu curse — Yirmeyahu 22:24 — where YHWH declared that even if Yekonyahu were a signet ring on YHWH's hand, YHWH would tear him off. Zerubbavel, the descendant of Yekonyahu, now receives the reverse covenant promise: he will be made the signet ring. The covenant curse on the Davidic line reaches its covenant reversal.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 9 — the latter glory of this house shall be greater than the former. And in this place I will give peace. The covenant covenant promise that transcends the covenant visible: the second temple in its current covenant form appears smaller and less glorious than Solomon's — but the covenant promise declares its covenant latter glory will exceed the former. The covenant word of YHWH establishes covenant reality that covenant present covenant appearances cannot measure.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 12-14 — the covenant priestly torah on holiness and uncleanness. The covenant principle established: holiness does not transmit by contact as easily as uncleanness does. The covenant application — verse 14 — so is it with this people: the covenant community's ritual offerings cannot sanctify the covenant community as long as the covenant community is in a state of covenant uncleanness. Covenant temple rebuilding alone does not produce covenant holiness — the covenant community itself must be covenant clean.

ZEKHARIYAH CHAPTERS 1 THROUGH 8 — THE EIGHT VISIONS AND THE COVENANT RESTORATION

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 1 Return to Me and I Will Return to You — The Man Among the Myrtle Trees — The Four Horns and the Four Craftsmen — YHWH Will Again Choose Yerushalayim

1 In the eighth month, in the second year of Darius, the word of YHWH came to Zekhariyahu the son of Berekhyahu, son of Iddo, the prophet, saying,

2 YHWH was very angry with your fathers.

3 Therefore say to them, Thus declares YHWH of hosts: Return to me, says YHWH of hosts, and I will return to you, says YHWH of hosts.

4 Do not be like your fathers, to whom the former prophets cried out, Thus says YHWH of hosts, Return from your evil ways and from your evil deeds. But they did not hear or pay attention to me, declares YHWH.

5 Your fathers, where are they? And the prophets, do they live forever?

6 But my words and my statutes, which I commanded my servants the prophets, did they not overtake your fathers? So they repented and said, As YHWH of hosts purposed to deal with us for our ways and deeds, so has he dealt with us.

7 On the twenty-fourth day of the eleventh month, which is the month of Shevat, in the second year of Darius, the word of YHWH came to the prophet Zekhariyahu the son of Berekhyahu, son of Iddo, saying:

8 I saw in the night, and behold, a man riding on a red horse. He was standing among the myrtle trees in the glen, and behind him were red, sorrel, and white horses.

9 Then I said, What are these, my lord? The angel who talked with me said to me, I will show you what they are.

10 So the man who was standing among the myrtle trees answered, These are they whom YHWH has sent to patrol the earth.

11 And they answered the angel of YHWH who was standing among the myrtle trees, and said, We have patrolled the earth, and behold, all the earth remains at rest.

12 Then the angel of YHWH said, O YHWH of hosts, how long will you have no mercy on Yerushalayim and the cities of Yehudah, against which you have been angry these seventy years?

13 And YHWH answered gracious and comforting words to the angel who talked with me.

14 So the angel who talked with me said to me, Cry out, Thus says YHWH of hosts: I am exceedingly jealous for Yerushalayim and for Tziyon.

15 And I am exceedingly angry with the nations that are at ease. For while I was angry but a little, they furthered the disaster.

16 Therefore, thus says YHWH, I have returned to Yerushalayim with mercy. My house shall be built in it, declares YHWH of hosts, and the measuring line shall be stretched out over Yerushalayim.

17 Cry out again, Thus says YHWH of hosts: My cities shall again overflow with prosperity, and YHWH will again comfort Tziyon and again choose Yerushalayim.

18 And I lifted my eyes and saw, and behold, four horns.

19 And I said to the angel who talked with me, What are these? And he said to me, These are the horns that have scattered Yehudah, Yisra'EL, and Yerushalayim.

20 Then YHWH showed me four craftsmen.

21 And I said, What are these coming to do? He said, These are the horns that scattered Yehudah, so that no one raised his head. And these craftsmen have come to terrify them, to cast down the horns of the nations who lifted up their horns against the land of Yehudah to scatter it.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Zekhariyah in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 1 opens the most vision-intensive book in the Twelve: eight night visions in Chapters 1-6 form the covenant apocalyptic framework of the book. The man among the myrtle trees in the glen — verse 8 — is the covenant patrol report of YHWH's covenant agents: the earth is at rest while the covenant people are still in distress. The covenant disproportion produces the covenant intercession of the covenant angel of YHWH.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 1 in full. Verse 3 — return to me, says YHWH of hosts, and I will return to you. The covenant reciprocity of covenant return: YHWH's covenant return is conditioned on and simultaneous with the covenant community's covenant return. The covenant distance between YHWH and the covenant community is symmetrical: the covenant initiative of return must come from both sides. YHWH does not covenant return unilaterally — he waits for the covenant community's covenant turning.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 1. Verse 15 — I am exceedingly angry with the nations that are at ease. For while I was angry but a little, they furthered the disaster. The covenant over-fulfillment indictment of the covenant nations: YHWH authorized a covenant measured discipline of the covenant people, and the covenant nations exceeded the covenant mandate — they used the covenant authorization as an opportunity for covenant cruelty beyond what YHWH intended.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5-6 — your fathers, where are they? And the prophets, do they live forever? But my words and my statutes, did they not overtake your fathers? The covenant argument from covenant historical evidence: the fathers are gone, the prophets are gone, but the covenant words overtook them — caught up with them in fulfillment. The covenant word outlasts the covenant messenger and the covenant recipient. The covenant post-exilic community is living covenant proof that the former covenant prophets' covenant words were covenant true.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 2 A Man With a Measuring Line — Yerushalayim Shall Be Inhabited as Villages — I Will Be the Glory Within Her — Flee From the Land of the North — YHWH Will Inherit Yehudah as His Portion

1 And I lifted my eyes and saw, and behold, a man with a measuring line in his hand.

2 Then I said, Where are you going? And he said to me, To measure Yerushalayim, to see what is its width and what is its length.

3 And behold, the angel who talked with me came forward, and another angel came forward to meet him

4 And said to him, Run, say to that young man, Yerushalayim shall be inhabited as villages without walls, because of the multitude of people and livestock in it.

5 And I will be to her a wall of fire all around, declares YHWH, and I will be the glory in her midst.

6 Up. Up. Flee from the land of the north, declares YHWH. For I have spread you abroad as the four winds of the heavens, declares YHWH.

7 Up. Escape to Tziyon, you who dwell with the daughter of Babylon.

8 For thus said YHWH of hosts, after his glory sent me to the nations who plundered you, for he who touches you touches the apple of his eye:

9 Behold, I will shake my hand over them, and they shall become plunder for those who served them. Then you will know that YHWH of hosts has sent me.

10 Sing and rejoice, O daughter of Tziyon, for behold, I come and I will dwell in your midst, declares YHWH.

11 And many nations shall join themselves to YHWH in that day, and shall be my people. And I will dwell in your midst, and you shall know that YHWH of hosts has sent me to you.

12 And YHWH will inherit Yehudah as his portion in the holy land, and will again choose Yerushalayim.

13 Be silent, all flesh, before YHWH, for he has roused himself from his holy dwelling.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 2 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The vision of the man with the measuring line — parallel to the measuring man in Yehezkel 40 — is interrupted by the covenant announcement that the covenant measuring plan must be revised: Yerushalayim's covenant future population will overflow all covenant walls. The covenant city of the restoration cannot be contained within conventional covenant architectural parameters.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 2 in full. Verse 11 — many nations shall join themselves to YHWH in that day, and shall be my people. The covenant universalism of the covenant restoration: the covenant community that returns to Yerushalayim will not be ethnically restricted — many nations will join YHWH and become his covenant people. The covenant land is the covenant magnet that draws covenant peoples from the covenant nations.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 2. Verse 8 — he who touches you touches the apple of his eye. The covenant protection declaration using the most intimate covenant physical metaphor: the apple of the eye — the pupil — is the most protected part of the human body, instinctively defended. YHWH's covenant protective instinct toward the covenant people is as immediate and involuntary as the eye's covenant reflex to protect the pupil.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 5 — I will be to her a wall of fire all around, and I will be the glory in her midst. The covenant architectural revolution: the covenant city of the future does not need covenant defensive walls because YHWH himself is the covenant wall of fire. The covenant protection that Yerushalayim previously sought through covenant fortifications is replaced by YHWH's covenant presence as the covenant perimeter defense. The covenant dwelling of YHWH in the covenant city makes the covenant city covenant invulnerable.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 3 The High Priest Yehoshua — The Adversary — Remove the Filthy Garments — I Will Remove the Iniquity of This Land in a Single Day — The Stone With Seven Eyes — Under His Vine and Fig Tree

1 Then he showed me Yehoshua the high priest standing before the angel of YHWH, and the Adversary standing at his right hand to accuse him.

2 And YHWH said to the Adversary, YHWH rebuke you, O Adversary. YHWH who has chosen Yerushalayim rebuke you. Is not this a brand plucked from the fire?

3 Now Yehoshua was standing before the angel, clothed with filthy garments.

4 And the angel said to those who were standing before him, Remove the filthy garments from him. And to him he said, Behold, I have taken your iniquity away from you, and I will clothe you with pure vestments.

5 And I said, Let them put a clean turban on his head. So they put a clean turban on his head and clothed him with garments. And the angel of YHWH was standing by.

6 And the angel of YHWH solemnly assured Yehoshua,

7 Thus says YHWH of hosts: If you will walk in my ways and keep my charge, then you shall rule my house and have charge of my courts, and I will give you the right of access among those who are standing here.

8 Hear now, O Yehoshua the high priest, you and your friends who sit before you, for they are men who are a sign: behold, I will bring my servant the Branch.

9 For behold, on the stone that I have set before Yehoshua, on a single stone with seven eyes, I will engrave its inscription, declares YHWH of hosts, and I will remove the iniquity of this land in a single day.

10 In that day, declares YHWH of hosts, every one of you will invite his neighbor to come under his vine and under his fig tree.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 3 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The fourth vision — the high priest Yehoshua in filthy garments before the covenant angel — is the covenant cleansing of the post-exilic priesthood: the covenant accusation of the Adversary is overruled by YHWH's covenant rebuke, and the filthy covenant priestly garments are replaced with covenant pure vestments. The covenant priesthood is covenantally restored through covenant forensic acquittal, not covenant moral achievement.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 3 in full. Verse 9 — I will remove the iniquity of this land in a single day. The covenant compressed time of covenant iniquity-removal: the covenant work that takes the covenant community decades of covenant effort is accomplished by YHWH in a single covenant day. The covenant stone with seven eyes — seven being the covenant completeness number — is the covenant symbol of YHWH's covenant all-seeing covenant superintendence of the covenant restoration.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 3. Verse 8 — I will bring my servant the Branch. The covenant messianic title Tzemakh — the Branch — appears in Yirmeyahu 23:5 and 33:15 as the covenant designation of the coming Davidic covenant ruler. Here in Zekhariyah it is introduced in the context of the priestly covenant restoration: the covenant Branch will come in the context of the covenant priestly covenant cleansing.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 2 — is not this a brand plucked from the fire? The covenant image of the covenant community as a partially burned stick rescued from the covenant fire: the exile was the covenant fire from which Yehoshua represents the covenant community. The covenant accusation of the Adversary against Yehoshua's covenant fitness for covenant priestly service is answered by YHWH with the covenant image of rescue: the brand plucked from the fire is not covenant evaluated by its covenant burned condition but by the covenant act of covenant rescue that brought it out.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 4 The Golden Lampstand — Two Olive Trees — Not by Might Nor by Power But by My Spirit — Who Has Despised the Day of Small Things — The Two Anointed Ones

1 And the angel who talked with me came again and woke me, like a man who is awakened out of his sleep.

2 And he said to me, What do you see? I said, I see, and behold, a lampstand all of gold, with a bowl on the top of it, and seven lamps on it, with seven lips on each of the lamps that are on the top of it.

3 And there are two olive trees by it, one on the right of the bowl and the other on its left.

4 And I said to the angel who talked with me, What are these, my lord?

5 Then the angel who talked with me answered and said to me, Do you not know what these are? I said, No, my lord.

6 Then he said to me, This is the word of YHWH to Zerubbavel: Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit, says YHWH of hosts.

7 Who are you, O great mountain? Before Zerubbavel you shall become a plain. And he shall bring forward the top stone amid shouts of Grace, grace to it.

8 Then the word of YHWH came to me, saying,

9 The hands of Zerubbavel have laid the foundation of this house. His hands shall also complete it. Then you will know that YHWH of hosts has sent me to you.

10 For whoever has despised the day of small things shall rejoice, and shall see the plumb line in the hand of Zerubbavel. These seven are the eyes of YHWH, which range through the whole earth.

11 Then I said to him, What are these two olive trees on the right and the left of the lampstand?

12 And a second time I answered and said to him, What are these two branches of the olive trees, which are beside the two golden pipes from which the golden oil is poured out?

13 He said to me, Do you not know what these are? I said, No, my lord.

14 Then he said, These are the two anointed ones who stand by the Sovereign of the whole earth.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 4 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The fifth vision — the golden lampstand and the two olive trees — is the covenant architectural vision of the covenant restoration's power source: the lampstand fed by the two olive trees without human intervention. Verse 6 — not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit — is the covenant declaration that the covenant restoration is not a covenant human achievement but a covenant divine operation through covenant Spirit-power.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 4 in full. Verse 14 — the two anointed ones who stand by the Sovereign of the whole earth. The covenant identification of the two anointed ones — Zerubbavel the governor and Yehoshua the high priest — as the covenant double-anointing of the covenant restoration: covenant royal and covenant priestly authority together before the covenant universal Sovereign. The covenant two streams of covenant anointed leadership flow into the covenant lampstand of the covenant community.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 4. Verse 10 — whoever has despised the day of small things shall rejoice. The covenant encouragement to the covenant community discouraged by the covenant modest scale of the second temple compared to the covenant former glory of the first: the covenant day of small beginnings is not a covenant failure but a covenant starting point. The covenant plumb line in Zerubbavel's hand is the covenant sign that the covenant work will be completed.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6 — not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit. The covenant principle stated at the covenant midpoint of the eight visions: the covenant restoration project is not achievable through covenant human resources — military might, political power — but only through the covenant Spirit of YHWH. The same covenant principle that undergirds the covenant proclamation of Havakuk 3:19 is here stated as the covenant architectural principle of the covenant temple rebuilding. Covenant outcomes that exceed covenant human capacity require covenant Spirit enablement.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 5 The Flying Scroll — The Curse That Goes Out Over the Face of the Whole Land — The Woman in the Basket — Wickedness — Carried to the Land of Shinar

1 Again I lifted my eyes and saw, and behold, a flying scroll.

2 And he said to me, What do you see? I answered, I see a flying scroll. Its length is twenty cubits, and its width ten cubits.

3 Then he said to me, This is the curse that goes out over the face of the whole land. For everyone who steals shall be cleaned out according to what is on one side, and everyone who swears falsely shall be cleaned out according to what is on the other side.

4 I will send it out, declares YHWH of hosts, and it shall enter the house of the thief, and the house of him who swears falsely by my name. And it shall remain in his house and consume it, both timber and stones.

5 Then the angel who talked with me came forward and said to me, Lift your eyes and see what this is that is going out.

6 And I said, What is it? He said, This is the basket that is going out. And he said, This is their iniquity in all the land.

7 And behold, the leaden cover was lifted, and there was a woman sitting in the basket.

8 And he said, This is Wickedness. And he thrust her back into the basket, and thrust down the leaden weight on its opening.

9 Then I lifted my eyes and saw, and behold, two women coming forward. The wind was in their wings. They had wings like the wings of a stork, and they lifted up the basket between earth and heaven.

10 Then I said to the angel who talked with me, Where are they taking the basket?

11 He said to me, To the land of Shinar, to build a house for it. And when this is prepared, they will set the basket down there on its base.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 5 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The sixth vision — the flying scroll — is the covenant curse that executes covenant judgment on covenant thieves and covenant false-swearers within the covenant restored community. The covenant seventh vision — the woman in the basket — is the covenant removal of wickedness from the covenant land to Shinar — Babylon. The covenant restoration is not merely covenant gathering but covenant cleansing: covenant evil is covenant removed and covenant transported away.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 5 in full. Verse 11 — to the land of Shinar, to build a house for it. The covenant geographic irony: wickedness — the covenant force that empowered Babylon — is returned to Babylon. The covenant origin of covenant wickedness is returned to its covenant geographical home. The covenant land of Yisra'EL is covenantally cleansed by the covenant removal of wickedness to the covenant land of its covenant origin.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 5. Verse 3 — the curse that goes out over the face of the whole land — on one side against thieves, on the other against false swearers. The covenant scroll of the curse covers the two foundational covenant violations: theft — covenant violation of the seventh covenant commandment's extension — and false swearing — covenant violation of the third covenant commandment. The covenant restoration community must be covenant clean of both.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 8 — this is Wickedness. And he thrust her back into the basket. The covenant personification of wickedness as a woman thrust back into a container is the covenant forcible removal of the covenant corruption from the covenant land. The covenant seal — the leaden cover — prevents covenant escape. The covenant restoration cannot coexist with covenant wickedness in the covenant land: one or the other must be removed. YHWH's covenant answer is to remove the wickedness rather than the covenant people.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 6 The Four Chariots — They Go Out to the Four Winds of Heaven — The Crown Upon the Head of Yehoshua — He Shall Build the Temple of YHWH — He Shall Bear Royal Honor — A Man Whose Name Is the Branch

1 Again I lifted my eyes and saw, and behold, four chariots came out from between two mountains. And the mountains were mountains of bronze.

2 The first chariot had red horses, the second black horses,

3 The third white horses, and the fourth chariot dappled horses — all of them strong.

4 Then I answered and said to the angel who talked with me, What are these, my lord?

5 And the angel answered and said to me, These are going out to the four winds of heaven, after presenting themselves before the Sovereign of all the earth.

6 The chariot with the black horses goes toward the north country, the white ones go after them, and the dappled ones go toward the south country.

7 When the strong horses came out, they were impatient to go and patrol the earth. And he said, Go, patrol the earth. So they patrolled the earth.

8 Then he cried to me, Behold, those who go toward the north country have set my Spirit at rest in the north country.

9 And the word of YHWH came to me:

10 Take from the exiles Heldai, Toviyahu, and Yeda'yahu, who have arrived from Babylon, and go the same day to the house of Yoshiyahu the son of Tzefanyahu.

11 Take from them silver and gold, and make a crown, and set it on the head of Yehoshua, the son of Yehotzadak, the high priest.

12 And say to him, Thus says YHWH of hosts, Behold, the man whose name is the Branch. For he shall branch out from his place, and he shall build the temple of YHWH.

13 It is he who shall build the temple of YHWH and shall bear royal honor, and shall sit and rule on his throne. And there shall be a priest on his throne, and the counsel of peace shall be between them both.

14 And the crown shall be in the temple of YHWH as a reminder to Helem, Toviyahu, Yeda'yahu, and Hen the son of Tzefanyahu.

15 And those who are far off shall come and help to build the temple of YHWH. And you shall know that YHWH of hosts has sent me to you. And this shall come to pass, if you will diligently obey the voice of YHWH your Creator.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 6 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The eighth and final night vision — the four chariots — mirrors the first vision of Chapter 1: the patrol horses return as the divine chariot-forces that go out to the four winds executing YHWH's covenant governance. The covenant vision cycle is complete. The covenant coronation of Yehoshua that follows — verses 9-15 — is the covenant acted prophecy of the coming Branch who will bear both covenant priestly and covenant royal honor.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 6 in full. Verse 13 — he shall sit and rule on his throne, and there shall be a priest on his throne, and the counsel of peace shall be between them both. The covenant constitutional innovation of the coming covenant order: covenant royal and covenant priestly authority are not merged into one person but held by two who counsel together in peace. The covenant tension between covenant king and covenant priest that plagued the covenant monarchy is resolved by covenant peaceful covenant counsel.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 6. Verse 12 — the man whose name is the Branch. For he shall branch out from his place, and he shall build the temple of YHWH. The covenant name Tzemakh — the Branch — now attached to the coming covenant builder of the covenant temple. The covenant temple being built by Zerubbavel in Zekhariyah's own day anticipates the covenant temple that the Branch will build in the covenant future. The covenant present covenant work is the covenant type of the covenant future covenant fulfillment.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 15 — those who are far off shall come and help to build the temple of YHWH. And this shall come to pass, if you will diligently obey the voice of YHWH your Creator. The covenant conditionality embedded within the covenant promise: the covenant far-off participation in the covenant temple building is contingent on the covenant community's covenant obedience to YHWH's voice. The covenant promise is real but not unconditional — it requires covenant faithful covenant response to activate the covenant fulfillment.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 7 Should I Weep in the Fifth Month — The Former Prophets Cried Out — They Refused to Pay Attention — They Made Their Hearts Diamond Hard — They Scattered Them Among the Nations

1 In the fourth year of King Darius, the word of YHWH came to Zekhariyahu on the fourth day of the ninth month, which is Kislev.

2 Now the people of Beit-El had sent Sharezer and Regem-Melekh and their men to entreat the favor of YHWH,

3 And to ask the priests of the house of YHWH of hosts and the prophets, Should I weep and abstain in the fifth month, as I have done for so many years?

4 Then the word of YHWH of hosts came to me:

5 Say to all the people of the land and the priests, When you fasted and mourned in the fifth month and in the seventh, for these seventy years, was it for me that you fasted?

6 And when you eat and when you drink, do you not eat for yourselves and drink for yourselves?

7 Were not these the words that YHWH proclaimed by the former prophets, when Yerushalayim was inhabited and prosperous, with her cities around her, and the Negev and the lowland were inhabited?

8 And the word of YHWH came to Zekhariyahu, saying,

9 Thus says YHWH of hosts, Render true judgments, show kindness and mercy to one another,

10 Do not oppress the widow, the fatherless, the sojourner, or the poor, and let none of you devise evil against another in your heart.

11 But they refused to pay attention and turned a stubborn shoulder and stopped their ears that they might not hear.

12 They made their hearts diamond hard lest they should hear the Torah and the words that YHWH of hosts had sent by his Spirit through the former prophets. Therefore great anger came from YHWH of hosts.

13 As I called, and they would not hear, so they called, and I would not hear, says YHWH of hosts,

14 And I scattered them with a whirlwind among all the nations that they had not known. Thus the land they left was desolate, so that no one went to and fro, and the pleasant land was made desolate.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 7 in close agreement with the Masoretic. The covenant question about the covenant fast — should we continue fasting in the fifth month commemorating the temple's destruction? — receives the covenant answer that reframes the entire covenant fast tradition: the covenant fasts were never truly for YHWH but for the covenant fasters themselves — verse 5-6. The covenant answer the covenant community needs is not a covenant fast calendar but covenant justice, kindness, and mercy — verse 9-10.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 7 in full. Verse 12 — they made their hearts diamond hard lest they should hear the Torah and the words that YHWH of hosts had sent by his Spirit through the former prophets. The covenant progressive hardening: the fathers' hearts became diamond — shamir — the hardest stone — through sustained covenant refusal to hear. The covenant hardening was not YHWH's covenant act but the covenant community's sustained covenant choice.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 7. Verse 13 — as I called and they would not hear, so they called and I would not hear. The covenant symmetry of covenant communication breakdown: the covenant refusal to hear YHWH's covenant word produces covenant deafness to the covenant community's covenant prayer. The covenant communication channels are covenant bilateral: closed from one direction, they close in both.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verses 9-10 — render true judgments, show kindness and mercy to one another, do not oppress the widow, fatherless, sojourner, or poor, let none of you devise evil against another in your heart. The covenant ethical core that the covenant fasts were meant to express but replaced: the covenant ritual of fasting without the covenant substance of covenant justice and covenant kindness is the covenant problem the former prophets diagnosed and the post-exilic covenant community is still being warned against.

ZEKHARIYAH — CHAPTER 8 I Am Jealous for Tziyon — Old Men and Women Shall Again Sit in the Streets — I Will Save My People — Just as I Purposed to Bring Disaster — Ten Men Shall Take Hold of the Robe of a Jew

1 And the word of YHWH of hosts came, saying,

2 Thus says YHWH of hosts: I am jealous for Tziyon with great jealousy, and I am jealous for her with great wrath.

3 Thus says YHWH: I have returned to Tziyon and will dwell in the midst of Yerushalayim, and Yerushalayim shall be called the faithful city, and the mountain of YHWH of hosts, the holy mountain.

4 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Old men and old women shall again sit in the streets of Yerushalayim, each with staff in hand because of great age.

5 And the streets of the city shall be full of boys and girls playing in its streets.

6 Thus says YHWH of hosts: If it is marvelous in the sight of the remnant of this people in those days, should it also be marvelous in my sight, declares YHWH of hosts?

7 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Behold, I will save my people from the east country and from the west country,

8 And I will bring them to dwell in the midst of Yerushalayim. And they shall be my people, and I will be their Creator, in faithfulness and in righteousness.

9 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Let your hands be strong, you who in these days have been hearing these words from the mouth of the prophets who were present on the day that the foundation of the house of YHWH of hosts was laid, that the temple might be built.

10 For before those days there was no wage for man or any wage for beast, neither was there any safety from the foe for him who went out or came in, for I set every man against his neighbor.

11 But now I will not deal with the remnant of this people as in the former days, declares YHWH of hosts.

12 For there shall be a sowing of peace. The vine shall give its fruit, and the ground shall give its produce, and the heavens shall give their dew. And I will cause the remnant of this people to possess all these things.

13 And as you have been a byword of cursing among the nations, O house of Yehudah and house of Yisra'EL, so will I save you, and you shall be a blessing. Fear not, but let your hands be strong.

14 For thus says YHWH of hosts: As I purposed to bring disaster to you when your fathers provoked me to wrath, and I did not relent, says YHWH of hosts,

15 So again have I purposed in these days to bring good to Yerushalayim and to the house of Yehudah. Fear not.

16 These are the things that you shall do: Speak the truth to one another. Render in your gates judgments that are true and make for peace.

17 Do not devise evil in your hearts against one another, and love no false oath, for all these things I hate, declares YHWH.

18 And the word of YHWH of hosts came to me, saying,

19 Thus says YHWH of hosts: The fast of the fourth month and the fast of the fifth and the fast of the seventh and the fast of the tenth shall be to the house of Yehudah seasons of joy and gladness and cheerful feasts. Therefore love truth and peace.

20 Thus says YHWH of hosts: Peoples shall yet come, even the inhabitants of many cities.

21 The inhabitants of one city shall go to another, saying, Let us go at once to entreat the favor of YHWH and to seek YHWH of hosts. I myself am going.

22 Many peoples and strong nations shall come to seek YHWH of hosts in Yerushalayim and to entreat the favor of YHWH.

23 Thus says YHWH of hosts: In those days ten men from the nations of every tongue shall take hold of the robe of a Yehudi, saying, Let us go with you, for we have heard that Creator is with you.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] 4QXII preserves Chapter 8 in close agreement with the Masoretic. Chapter 8 is the covenant restoration counterpart to Chapter 7's covenant historical recitation of judgment: the same covenant precision — thus says YHWH of hosts, repeated ten times — that pronounced covenant judgment is now applied to covenant restoration. The covenant community that was a byword of cursing will become a blessing. The covenant fasts of mourning become covenant feasts of joy.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. Verse 23 — ten men from the nations of every tongue shall take hold of the robe of a Yehudi, saying, Let us go with you, for we have heard that Creator is with you. The covenant universalism of the covenant restoration's magnetic covenant effect: the covenant restoration of the covenant community draws the nations not by covenant coercion but by covenant reputation — we have heard that Creator is with you.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic and DSS are in close agreement for Chapter 8. Verses 4-5 — old men and old women shall again sit in the streets of Yerushalayim, and the streets shall be full of boys and girls playing. The covenant vision of the covenant restored city expressed in the most domestic and human of covenant images: the elderly safe in the streets and children playing freely. The covenant shalom of the covenant restored city is measured not in covenant architectural grandeur but in covenant human flourishing at every age.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 14-15 — as I purposed to bring disaster when your fathers provoked me, and I did not relent — so again I have purposed to bring good. The covenant symmetry argument: the same covenant unchangeable covenant purpose that made covenant judgment inevitable now makes covenant restoration inevitable. The covenant community that struggled to believe in covenant restoration is reminded that the same covenant divine purposefulness that produced the covenant disaster is now directed toward covenant good. YHWH's covenant purpose is the covenant anchor of both covenant judgment and covenant hope.

END OF PART FOUR — HAVAKUK · TZEFANYAH · KHAGAI · ZEKHARIYAH 1-8

𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄
